launchScreen.title: PC ARCHITECT
launchScreen.hint: Press <kbd>F11</kbd> or <kbd>Esc</kbd> to toggle fullscreen anytime

scenarioSelect.title: PC ARCHITECT
scenarioSelect.subtitle: Choose your mission. Build the perfect PC.

office.title: OFFICE
office.description: Build a reliable office workstation for everyday productivity, emails, and document editing.

gaming.title: GAMING
gaming.description: Build the ultimate gaming rig that crushes AAA titles at high settings and competitive FPS.

workstation.title: WORKSTATION
workstation.description: Build a powerhouse for 3D rendering, video editing, and heavy multi-threaded workloads.

configurator.confirmReset: Are you sure you want to reset your entire build? The timer will keep running!
configurator.hintTooltip: Get a sarcastic nudge from Walter
configurator.assembleBuildTooltip: All required parts are chosen and compatible — build it!
configurator.assembleNotReady: Select all required components with no conflicts first

results.loadingSubtitle: Hmm, let me analyze this build...

great.roast: No roasts today, you actually did well.
great.analysis: Everything is compatible, the performance is top-notch, and you stayed in budget.
great.tip: Maybe try building blindfolded next time.

decent.roast: Your build works but it's like putting ketchup on a steak.
decent.analysis: Functional, but could be better. Check your component balance and budget usage.
decent.tip: Review the CPU to GPU pairing to ensure neither is bottlenecking the other.

disaster.roast: I've seen potatoes with better calculation logic than your part selection.
disaster.analysis: There are serious compatibility issues, budget problems, or missing components.
disaster.tip: Start from scratch and maybe read the requirements this time.

walter_hints.conflict: Fix the fire in the conflict list first before begging for my wisdom.
walter_hints.missing_crucial: You burned almost the entire budget and still don’t have {{partName}}. Brilliant planning—just not in the good way.
walter_hints.cpu_bottleneck: Your GPU is ready for esports while the CPU is still doing spreadsheets. Maybe upgrade the brain, not just the biceps.
walter_hints.gpu_bottleneck: Your CPU is a beast, your GPU is a toaster. Maybe give your frames a fighting chance.
walter_hints.weak_workstation: For a workstation this looks more like an intern’s PC. Put some budget into CPU and RAM.
walter_hints.overspent_looks: You spent more on metal and fans than on actual performance. Maybe the PC should do more than just look pretty.
walter_hints.hoarding_budget: You’re hoarding budget like a dragon and still running entry-level parts. Spend a bit on a real CPU or GPU.
walter_hints.no_core: Bold move: a PC without a PC. Start with CPU, RAM, and GPU before you decorate.
walter_hints.solid: Looks like a solid work-in-progress. A couple of focused upgrades and even Walter might stop complaining.

parts.gpu: a GPU or any display output

compat.socketMismatch: Socket mismatch: {{cpu}} ({{cpuSocket}}) is not compatible with {{mb}} ({{mbSocket}})
compat.coolerMount: Cooler mounting issue: {{cooler}} does not include brackets for {{socket}} socket
compat.noDisplay: No Display Output: {{cpu}} does not have integrated graphics. You must add a dedicated GPU.
compat.ramTypeMismatch: RAM type mismatch: {{ram}} ({{ramType}}) does not match {{mb}} ({{mbRamType}})
compat.insufficientCooling: Insufficient cooling: {{cooler}} is rated for {{coolerTdp}}W, but {{cpu}} has a TDP of {{cpuTdp}}W
compat.insufficientPsu: Insufficient PSU: {{psu}} ({{psuWattage}}W) may not handle peak draw of {{draw}}W (TDP: {{tdp}}W)
compat.bottleneckCpuOverGpu: Severe Bottleneck: The powerful {{cpu}} is severely bottlenecked by the {{gpuTier}}-tier {{gpu}}.
compat.bottleneckGpuOverCpu: Severe Bottleneck: The powerful {{gpu}} is held back by the {{cpuTier}}-tier {{cpu}}.
compat.caseMbMismatch: Case mismatch: {{pcCase}} does not support {{formFactor}} motherboards.
compat.gpuTooLong: GPU too long: {{gpu}} is {{gpuLength}}mm, but {{pcCase}} only supports up to {{maxLength}}mm.
compat.coolerTooTall: Cooler too tall: {{cooler}} is {{coolerHeight}}mm, but {{pcCase}} only supports up to {{maxHeight}}mm.
